ogTitle: Towaninny vs Towaninny South: Climate Safety

heading: Towaninny vs Towaninny South — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Towaninny, Victoria and Towaninny South, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Towaninny South has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Towaninny (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Towaninny or Towaninny South safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Towaninny has a flood risk score of 53/100, while Towaninny South scores 47/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Towaninny scores 36/100 for bushfire risk. Towaninny South scores 41/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Towaninny or Towaninny South?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Towaninny South has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.82% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
